title: Is SoundHound Stock Still a Strong AI Play After the Q1 Selloff?
text: SoundHound AI stock experienced volatility following Q1 2026 earnings announcement despite strong revenue performance. The company reported Q1 revenue growth of 52% to $44.2 million, with core automotive and IoT AI revenue growing 88% organically excluding acquisitions. Despite strong top-line results, investors remain concerned about profitability metrics and cash burn. The company ended Q1 with $216 million in cash and zero debt, providing financial flexibility for continued operations and strategic initiatives. SoundHound's LivePerson acquisition is expected to close in the second half of 2026, positioning the combined company for significant revenue growth. Analysts maintain mixed views on the stock, with some highlighting the strong growth trajectory and market opportunities while others caution about execution risks and valuation concerns.
